item.title: Widespread Microsoft Entra lockouts tied to new security feature rollout
item.author: Lawrence Abrams
item.content:encoded: Windows administrators from numerous organizations report widespread account lockouts triggered by false positives in the rollout of a new Microsoft Entra ID's "leaked credentials" detection app called MACE. [...]

item.title: New Android malware steals your credit cards for NFC relay attacks
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: A new malware-as-a-service (MaaS) platform named 'SuperCard X' has emerged, targeting Android devices via NFC relay attacks that enable point-of-sale and ATM transactions using compromised payment card data. [...]

item.title: Rogue npm Packages Mimic Telegram Bot API to Plant SSH Backdoors on Linux Systems
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded:
Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered three malicious packages in the npm registry that masquerade as a popular Telegram bot library but harbor SSH backdoors and data exfiltration capabilities. The packages in question are listed below -
node-telegram-utils (132 downloads) node-telegram-bots-api (82 downloads) node-telegram-util (73 downloads)
According to supply chain

item.title: Critical Erlang/OTP SSH RCE bug now has public exploits, patch now
item.author: Lawrence Abrams
item.content:encoded: Public exploits are now available for a critical Erlang/OTP SSH vulnerability tracked as CVE-2025-32433, allowing unauthenticated attackers to remotely execute code on impacted devices. [...]

item.title: Google Gemini AI is getting ChatGPT-like Scheduled Actions feature
item.author: Mayank Parmar
item.content:encoded: Google Gemini is testing a ChatGPT-like scheduled tasks feature called "Scheduled Actions," which will allow you to create tasks that Gemini will execute later. [...]

item.title: ASUS Confirms Critical Flaw in AiCloud Routers; Users Urged to Update Firmware
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded:
ASUS has disclosed a critical security flaw impacting routers with AiCloud enabled that could permit remote attackers to perform unauthorized execution of functions on susceptible devices. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2025-2492, has a CVSS score of 9.2 out of a maximum of 10.0.
"An improper authentication control vulnerability exists in certain ASUS router firmware series,"

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Live Colossal Squid Filmed
item.author: Bruce Schneier
item.content:encoded: A live colossal squid was filmed for the firsttime in the ocean. It’s only a juvenile: a foot long.As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered.

item.title: Interlock ransomware gang pushes fake IT tools in ClickFix attacks
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: The Interlock ransomware gang now uses ClickFix attacks that impersonate IT tools to breach corporate networks and deploy file-encrypting malware on devices. [...]

item.title: OpenAI details ChatGPT-o3, o4-mini, o4-mini-high usage limits
item.author: Mayank Parmar
item.content:encoded: OpenAI has launched three new reasoning models - o3, o4-mini, and o4-mini-high for Plus and Pro subscribers, but as it turns out, these models do not offer 'unlimited' usage. [...]

item.title: FBI: Scammers pose as FBI IC3 employees to 'help' recover lost funds
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: The FBI warns that scammers posing as FBI IC3 employees are offering to "help" fraud victims recover money lost to other scammers. [...]

item.title: ASUS warns of critical auth bypass flaw in routers using AiCloud
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: ASUS is warning about an authentication bypass vulnerability in routers with AiCloud enabled that could allow remote attackers to perform unauthorized execution of functions on the device. [...]

item.title: Chinese Smishing Kit Powers Widespread Toll Fraud Campaign Targeting U.S. Users in 8 States
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Cybersecurity researchers are warning of a "widespread and ongoing" SMS phishing campaign that's been targeting toll road users in the United States for financial theft since mid-October 2024. "The toll road smishing attacks are being carried out by multiple financially motivated threat actors using the smishing kit developed by 'Wang Duo Yu,'" Cisco Talos researchers Azim Khodjibaev, Chetan

item.title: SonicWall SMA VPN devices targeted in attacks since January
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: A remote code execution vulnerability affecting SonicWall Secure Mobile Access (SMA) appliances has been under active exploitation since at least January 2025, according to cybersecurity company Arctic Wolf. [...]

item.title: Chinese hackers target Russian govt with upgraded RAT malware
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: Chinese-speaking IronHusky hackers are targeting Russian and Mongolian government organizations using upgraded MysterySnail remote access trojan (RAT) malware. [...]

item.title: 7 Steps to Take After a Credential-Based cyberattack
item.author: Sponsored by Specops Software
item.content:encoded: Hackers don't break in—they log in. Credential-based attacks now fuel nearly half of all breaches. Learn how to scan your Active Directory for compromised passwords and stop attackers before they strike. [...]

item.title: Cisco Webex bug lets hackers gain code execution via meeting links
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: Cisco has released security updates for a high-severity Webex vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to gain client-side remote code execution using malicious meeting invite links. [...]

item.title: Multi-Stage Malware Attack Uses .JSE and PowerShell to Deploy Agent Tesla and XLoader
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: A new multi-stage attack has been observed delivering malware families like Agent Tesla variants, Remcos RAT, and XLoader. "Attackers increasingly rely on such complex delivery mechanisms to evade detection, bypass traditional sandboxes, and ensure successful payload delivery and execution," Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 researcher Saqib Khanzada said in a technical write-up of the campaign. The

item.title: [Webinar] AI Is Already Inside Your SaaS Stack — Learn How to Prevent the Next Silent Breach
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Your employees didn’t mean to expose sensitive data. They just wanted to move faster. So they used ChatGPT to summarize a deal. Uploaded a spreadsheet to an AI-enhanced tool. Integrated a chatbot into Salesforce. No big deal—until it is. If this sounds familiar, you're not alone. Most security teams are already behind in detecting how AI tools are quietly reshaping their SaaS environments. And

item.title: Experts Uncover New XorDDoS Controller, Infrastructure as Malware Expands to Docker, Linux, IoT
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Cybersecurity researchers are warning of continued risks posed by a distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) malware known as XorDDoS, with 71.3 percent of the attacks between November 2023 and February 2025 targeting the United States. "From 2020 to 2023, the XorDDoS trojan has increased significantly in prevalence," Cisco Talos researcher Joey Chen said in a Thursday analysis.

item.title: CVE-2025-24054 Under Active Attack—Steals NTLM Credentials on File Download
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added a medium-severity security flaw impacting Microsoft Windows to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, following reports of active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, assigned the CVE identifier CVE-2025-24054 (CVSS score: 6.5), is a Windows New Technology LAN Manager (NTLM) hash disclosure

item.title: Critical Erlang/OTP SSH pre-auth RCE is 'Surprisingly Easy' to exploit, patch now
item.author: Lawrence Abrams
item.content:encoded: A critical vulnerability in the Erlang/OTP SSH, tracked as CVE-2025-32433, has been disclosed that allows for unauthenticated remote code execution on vulnerable devices. [...]

item.title: Entertainment services giant Legends International discloses data breach
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: Entertainment venue management firm Legends International warns it suffered a data breach in November 2024, which has impacted employees and people who visited venues under its management. [...]

item.title: Windows NTLM hash leak flaw exploited in phishing attacks on governments
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: A Windows vulnerability that exposes NTLM hashes using .library-ms files is now actively exploited by hackers in phishing campaigns targeting government entities and private companies. [...]

item.title: Chrome extensions with 6 million installs have hidden tracking code
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: A set of 57 Chrome extensions with 6,000,000 users have been discovered with very risky capabilities, such as monitoring browsing behavior, accessing cookies for domains, and potentially executing remote scripts. [...]

item.title: Age Verification Using Facial Scans
item.author: Bruce Schneier
item.content:encoded: “We’re currently running tests in select regions to age-gate access to certain spaces or user settings,” a spokesperson for Discord said in a statement. “The information shared to power the age verification method is only used for the one-time age verification process and is not stored by Discord or our vendor. For Face Scan, the solution our vendor uses operates on-device, which means there is no collection of any biometric information when you scan your face. For ID verification, the scan of your ID is deleted upon verification.”I look forward to all the videos of people hacking this system using various disguises.

item.title: Mustang Panda Targets Myanmar With StarProxy, EDR Bypass, and TONESHELL Updates
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: The China-linked threat actor known as Mustang Panda has been attributed to a cyber attack targeting an unspecified organization in Myanmar with previously unreported tooling, highlighting continued effort by the threat actors to increase the sophistication and effectiveness of their malware. This includes updated versions of a known backdoor called TONESHELL, as well as a new lateral movement

item.title: Ahold Delhaize confirms data theft after INC ransomware claims attack
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: Food retail giant Ahold Delhaize confirms that data was stolen from its U.S. business systems during a November 2024 cyberattack. [...]

item.title: CTM360 Tracks Global Surge in SMS-Based Reward and Toll Scams
item.author: Sponsored by CTM360
item.content:encoded: Thousands tricked by fake reward & toll scam texts. CTM360 exposes PointyPhish & TollShark—SMS phishing campaigns powered by the Darcula PhaaS platform, with 5K+ domains stealing payment info worldwide. [...]

item.title: Microsoft: Office 2016 and Office 2019 reach end of support in October
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: ​​Microsoft has reminded customers that Office 2016 and Office 2019 will reach the end of extended support six months from now, on October 14, 2025. [...]

item.title: State-Sponsored Hackers Weaponize ClickFix Tactic in Targeted Malware Campaigns
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Multiple state-sponsored hacking groups from Iran, North Korea, and Russia have been found leveraging the increasingly popular ClickFix social engineering tactic to deploy malware over a three-month period from late 2024 through the beginning of 2025. The phishing campaigns adopting the strategy have been attributed to clusters tracked as TA427 (aka Kimsuky), TA450 (aka MuddyWater),

item.title: Artificial Intelligence – What's all the fuss?
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Talking about AI: Definitions Artificial Intelligence (AI) — AI refers to the simulation of human intelligence in machines, enabling them to perform tasks that typically require human intelligence, such as decision-making and problem-solving. AI is the broadest concept in this field, encompassing various technologies and methodologies, including Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning. Machine

item.title: CISA warns of increased breach risks following Oracle Cloud leak
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: On Wednesday, CISA warned of heightened breach risks after the compromise of legacy Oracle Cloud servers earlier this year and highlighted the significant threat to enterprise networks. [...]

item.title: New Windows Server emergency updates fix container launch issue
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: Microsoft has released emergency Windows Server updates to address a known issue preventing Windows containers from launching. [...]

item.title: Blockchain Offers Security Benefits – But Don't Neglect Your Passwords
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Blockchain is best known for its use in cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin, but it also holds significant applications for online authentication. As businesses in varying sectors increasingly embrace blockchain-based security tools, could the technology one day replace passwords? How blockchain works Blockchain is a secure way to maintain, encrypt, and exchange digital records of transactions.

item.title: Critical Erlang/OTP SSH Vulnerability (CVSS 10.0) Allows Unauthenticated Code Execution
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in the Erlang/Open Telecom Platform (OTP) SSH implementation that could permit an attacker to execute arbitrary code sans any authentication under certain conditions. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2025-32433, has been given the maximum CVSS score of 10.0. "The vulnerability allows an attacker with network access to an Erlang/OTP SSH server

item.title: Node.js Malware Campaign Targets Crypto Users with Fake Binance and TradingView Installers
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Microsoft is calling attention to an ongoing malvertising campaign that makes use of Node.js to deliver malicious payloads capable of information theft and data exfiltration. The activity, first detected in October 2024, uses lures related to cryptocurrency trading to trick users into installing a rogue installer from fraudulent websites that masquerade as legitimate software like Binance or

item.title: CISA tags SonicWall VPN flaw as actively exploited in attacks
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: On Wednesday, CISA warned federal agencies to secure their SonicWall Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 100 series appliances against attacks exploiting a high-severity remote code execution vulnerability. [...]

item.title: CISA Flags Actively Exploited Vulnerability in SonicWall SMA Devices
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Wednesday added a security flaw impacting SonicWall Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 100 Series gateways to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. The high-severity vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2021-20035 (CVSS score: 7.2), relates to a case of operating system command injection

item.title: Apple Patches Two Actively Exploited iOS Flaws Used in Sophisticated Targeted Attacks
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded:
Apple on Wednesday released security updates for iOS, iPadOS, macOS Sequoia, tvOS, and visionOS to address two security flaws that it said have come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerabilities in question are listed below -
CVE-2025-31200 (CVSS score: 7.5) - A memory corruption vulnerability in the Core Audio framework that could allow code execution when processing an audio
